title: hymenophyllum recurvum
intro: Hymenophyllum recurvum, also known as the recurved scale fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may provide health benefits such as wound healing and infection prevention. Hymenophyllum recurvum can be prepared into herbal infusions, tinctures, or salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
